gene_region.label: CYP2C9 transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical CYP2C9 genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window used by this app to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval begins.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: CYP2C9 encodes cytochrome P450 2C9, a polymorphic drug-metabolizing monooxygenase. This bundle emphasizes phase I drug oxidation, warfarin/NSAID/phenytoin pharmacogenetics, star-allele activity, and exposure biology.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local CYP2C9 bundle is pharmacogenomic drug metabolism oriented. Interpret markers through exact allele class, zygosity, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context rather than as stand-alone deterministic calls.
variant_effect_overview: CYP2C9 variants can alter or tag phase I drug oxidation, warfarin/NSAID/phenytoin pharmacogenetics, star-allele activity, and exposure biology; effect direction depends on allele class, tissue, dosage, and biological context.
Common markers, pharmacogenetic alleles, structural events, and high-impact pathogenic variants should be kept separate during interpretation.
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: CYP2C9 methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a phase I drug oxidation, warfarin/NSAID/phenytoin pharmacogenetics, star-allele activity, and exposure biology gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for CYP2C9.
Because CYP2C9 biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.
Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against phase I drug oxidation, warfarin/NSAID/phenytoin pharmacogenetics, star-allele activity, and exposure biology.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use CYP2C9 promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
Variant-plus-methylation review for phase I drug oxidation, warfarin/NSAID/phenytoin pharmacogenetics, star-allele activity, and exposure biology.

evidence.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000138109: CYP2C9 coordinate source

biorender_visuals.label: CYP2C9 biochemical figure starter
biorender_visuals.focus: CYP2C9: phase I drug oxidation, warfarin/NSAID/phenytoin pharmacogenetics, star-allele activity, and exposure biology
biorender_visuals.usage_note: BioRender template and icon-library search results are bundled as figure starters. Open the template link in BioRender, then add or replace the listed icons to make a gene-specific biochemical pathway image.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a CYP2C9 pharmacogenomic drug metabolism thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to phase I drug oxidation, warfarin/NSAID/phenytoin pharmacogenetics, star-allele activity, and exposure biology and CYP2C9 pharmacogenomics context, Warfarin and phenytoin dose-sensitivity biology. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support.

variant_records.clinical_interpretation: rs1799853 (CYP2C9*2) is curated as a CYP2C9 marker for CYP2C9 pharmacogenomics context, Warfarin and phenytoin dose-sensitivity biology. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the phase I drug oxidation, warfarin/NSAID/phenytoin pharmacogenetics, star-allele activity, and exposure biology pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Curated research and clinical-context marker; external clinical classification is required for diagnostic use.
functional_effects: Can modify or tag phase I drug oxidation, warfarin/NSAID/phenytoin pharmacogenetics, star-allele activity, and exposure biology.
Best interpreted with tissue, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context.
research_context: Use this marker as a CYP2C9 pathway-context signal, not as a stand-alone diagnosis.
Sequence, methylation, expression, and protein data may point in different directions and should be reconciled.
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: Pair rs1799853 (CYP2C9*2) with CYP2C9 methylation only as local regulatory context; methylation does not prove the variant's biochemical effect.
